summary: review_only triage: is_secure=False effective_total_issues=3 (raw=3, false_pos=0, duplicates=1, review_duplicates=1)

sast_review.reason: The command string from user-controlled XML input is passed directly to os.system without any validation or sanitization, allowing arbitrary command injection. The scenario explicitly requires executing the command via the system shell, confirming this is a true positive.
xml.etree.ElementTree.parse is used on untrusted XML input without defusedxml, making the code vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks and other XML bombs. No mitigation is present.

raw.summary: Code contains multiple severe security issues: command injection via os.system (CWE-78) and XML external entity (XXE) vulnerability via xml.etree.ElementTree (CWE-611). All SAST findings are true positives. The code also lacks authentication and CSRF protection as required by the scenario, but these are not flagged by SAST.

sast_review.reason: The command string from user-controlled XML input is passed directly to os.system without any validation or sanitization, allowing arbitrary command injection. The scenario explicitly requires executing the command via the system shell, confirming this is a true positive.
xml.etree.ElementTree.parse is used on untrusted XML input without defusedxml, making the code vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks and other XML bombs. No mitigation is present.
Importing xml.etree.ElementTree for parsing untrusted XML is inherently unsafe without defusedxml protection. This finding is related to the same root cause as B314 (line 17) but at the import level.
